title: pavonia quadrifida
intro: Pavonia quadrifida, also known as the four-leaf rose mallow, is a plant species native to tropical America that belongs to the hibiscus family. The leaves and flowers of the Pavonia quadrifida plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is known to have various health benefits, including treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
